content.full_text:
February 13, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Council
FPPC
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin,

I have recently assumed the position of Public Works Director/City Engineer for the City of Paso Robles (January 2, 1991). My previous employer, NCE (North Coast Engineering, Inc.) is also located in the City of Paso Robles and does civil engineering work for private clients and the City.

NCE regularly submits plans to the City for checking by the Engineering Division which is a division under my departmental control. In general, I have the authority to sign engineering plans for the City, make recommendations regarding development proposals and provide project inspection services.

I was an employee only of North Coast Engineering and was not a partner nor did I own any stock in the firm. I worked for NCE for approximately 5 years.

In summary, I need to know what actions I may or may not take relative to:

1. Existing City contracts with NCE for consultant services executed prior to my employment.
2. Contracting or recommending contracting for new engineering services where NCE may be providing a proposal.
3. Checking plans and/or signing plans submitted by NCE.
4. Commenting on projects submitted by NCE relative to the City's position.
5. Dealing with clients of NCE either on a project designed by NCE or a project on other matters not involving NCE.

February 13, 1991
Mr. Scott Hallabrin

6. The time frame involved if I am to refrain from taking actions relative to items 1-5.

Sincerely,

JOHN R. McCARTHY, P.E.
Director of Public Works

mp

dpwcorsp\fppccnft.int
